audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar staffs-and-budding-sign text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for twelve tribal staffs, Aaron's staff for Levi, overnight sign before testimony, buds/blossoms/almonds, preservation of the staff, and the people's fear. Anti-authoritarian safeguards required.

verses.restored: Speak to children of Israel and take from them one staff for each fathers' house, from all their leaders according to fathers' houses, twelve staffs; each man's name you shall write upon his staff.
Aaron's name you shall write upon staff of Levi, for one staff shall be for head of each fathers' house.
You shall place them in tent of meeting before testimony, where I meet with you.
It shall be that staff of man whom I choose shall bud, and I will cause murmurings of children of Israel against you to cease from before me.
Moses spoke to children of Israel, and all their leaders gave him one staff for each leader, one staff for each fathers' house, twelve staffs; staff of Aaron was among their staffs.
Moses placed staffs before YHWH in tent of testimony.
On next day Moses entered tent of testimony, and look, staff of Aaron for house of Levi had budded; it brought forth buds, blossomed blossoms, and ripened almonds.
Moses brought out all staffs from before YHWH to all children of Israel; they saw, and each man took his staff.
YHWH said to Moses, Return staff of Aaron before testimony, to be kept as sign for sons of rebellion, so you may finish their murmurings from before me, and they not die.
Moses did so; as YHWH commanded him, so he did.
Children of Israel said to Moses, Look, we perish, we are lost, all of us are lost!
Everyone drawing near, drawing near to dwelling-place of YHWH, dies. Are we completely to perish?

translation_notes: The twelve staffs represent tribal leadership before YHWH; Aaron's staff is one among them until the sign occurs.
The budding staff settles the specific dispute over priestly approach after Numbers 16; it does not make Aaron or later leaders immune from accountability.
Bud, blossom, and ripe almonds appear together as concentrated life imagery emerging from dead wood.
The people's final fear shows that the sign does not automatically restore trust or felt safety; chapter 18 responds with clearer boundaries and responsibilities.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 17 to silence criticism, demand unquestioning loyalty, or claim that religious office proves moral perfection. The staff is a specific narrative sign concerning priestly vocation, not a universal license for authoritarian leadership.
